title: Variables, Expressions, Equations, and Practical Formulas · Error Forensics
summary: Build reusable cost, distance, energy, and capacity formulas from stakeholder requirements. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a formula-driven planning calculator and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define variables with units and domains rather than treating letters as unlabeled blanks.
Substitute values into expressions with grouping, units, and intermediate reasonableness checks.
Solve one-variable linear equations while preserving equality at every transformation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Inequality Constraints evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Inequality Constraints assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Model limits and feasible ranges with inequalities, including sign reversal under negative scaling. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner algebra formulas. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Inequality Constraints, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a formula-driven planning calculator.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An inequality behaves exactly like an equation under every operation. This fails the Inequality Constraints contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Inequality Constraints applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model limits and feasible ranges with inequalities, including sign reversal under negative scaling.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Variable Meaning.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using model and limits plus specific case evidence.
